per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I don't get too lightheaded, doc. But yeah, I do sweat a bit when it's happening - my shirt gets all wet and cold, but that's about it.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, doc. Just my chest and a little bit of my face, like I said. But I do get this tingling sensation in my left arm during the really bad episodes. It's hard to describe, but it feels like pins and needles or something.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, actually... my ankles have been feeling a bit swollen lately. Not too much, just that they feel tighter than usual when I put on my shoes in the morning. It's nothing too major, but it's definitely noticeable.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I did get a little queasy during some of these episodes, doc. Nothing too severe, just a bit of a stomach turn, but it's gone once the chest tightness goes away. And like I said, my wife mentions that I've been getting a bit more irritable than usual, but I chalked that up to stress from work and not being able to drive as much as I want to.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Doc, I have a few things going on that might be relevant. I was told I had high blood pressure about 8 years ago, and I've been taking Amlodipine for it. But to be honest, I don't always take my medication like I'm supposed to because of my schedule on the road. And then there's my diabetes - I've been taking Metformin for a few years now, but I try to stick to that one pretty consistently. Oh, and I should mention that my dad had a heart attack when he was 52 and passed away from it, so I guess you could say I'm a bit worried about following in his footsteps.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Doc, yeah... my mom is still alive at 78, but she's got hypertension and type 2 diabetes, just like me. And then there's my father... I mentioned he had a heart attack when he was 52 and passed away from it. It's always been on my mind since I'm getting up there in age myself now.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Doc, yeah... I'd be lying if I said I didn't worry about my own health. With my dad passing away from a heart attack at 52, and now experiencing these chest pains myself, it's hard not to think about the possibility of something similar happening to me. It keeps me up at night, worrying about whether I'll be okay or not.
